coredata.dc:title: Sensitive and selective detection of Hg2+ in tap and canal water via self-enhanced ECL aptasensor based on NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Libo
Chen, Bainian
Luo, Lijun
Liu, Xiaohong
Bi, Xiaoya
You, Tianyan
coredata.dc:description: In the present study, a novel self-enhanced electrochemiluminescence (ECL) aptasensor which combined self-enhanced ECL composite as signal response element and aptamer as the specific recognition element was firstly proposed for the sensitive and selective detection of Hg2+. Innovatively, the luminophore Ru(bpy)3 2+-doped silica nanoparticles functionalized with 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (NH2–Ru@SiO2) and the co-reactant nitrogen doped graphene quantum dots (NGQDs) were bound together to form the self-enhanced ECL composite, NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs, by electrostatic adsorption. Satisfactorily, high and stable luminous efficiency of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs was obtained benefited from the short electron transfer distance and low energy loss of self-enhanced-typal ECL composite. For the fabrication of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection, the Hg2+ aptamer, of which one end was connected to NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs, and the other end was fixed on glass carbon electrode (GCE) surface through Au–S bond, was served as a bridge. Upon the addition of Hg2+, the aptamer was bent due to the formation of thymine-Hg2+-thymine (T-Hg2+-T) specific structure, which caused the self-enhanced ECL composite was close to the GCE surface. On this basis, a linearly enhanced ECL signal was acquired with the concentration of Hg2+ in the range of 5.0 × 10−11 M − 1.0 × 10−6 M with excellent selectivity, repeatability and stability in 3 min for each assay. In addition, the proposed aptasensor showed satisfying accuracy and practicability for Hg2+ analysis in tap and canal water verified by the inductively coupled plasma-mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) method.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Nitrogen doped graphene quantum dots
Self-enhanced electrochemiluminescence
Aptasensor
Hg2+

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs self-enhanced ECL composite
Modification of the self-enhanced ECL composite (NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs) with Hg2+ aptamer
Fabrication of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection
Morphological and structural characterization of self-enhanced ECL composite
Fabrication of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor and feasibility study for Hg2+ detection
Analytical performance of the self-enhanced ECL aptaseneor for Hg2+ detection
Analysis of Hg2+ in tap and the grand canal water

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) TEM image of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs. (B) Zeta potential, (C) The UV–Vis absorption spectra and (D) fluorescence spectra of NGQDs (a), Ru@SiO2 (b), NH2–Ru@SiO2 (c) and NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs (d).

(A) CVs and (B) EIS curves of bare GCE (a), AuNPs/GCE (b), NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt/AuNPs/GCE (c) and BSA/NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt/AuNPs/GCE (d) in 5 mM [Fe(CN)6]3-/4- solution containing 0.2 M KCl. (C) ECL response of bare GCE (a), AuNPs/GCE (b), NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt/AuNPs/GCE (c), BSA/NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt/AuNPs/GCE (d) and Hg2+/BSA/NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt/AuNPs/GCE (e) in 0.1 M PBS (pH = 7.5). The scan rate is 100 mV s−1 in the CV graph. The frequency varied from 0.1 Hz to 100 kHz, 0 V bias potential and 5 mV amplitude of sine voltage signal was used in the EIS graph.

Optimization of (A) electrodeposition time, (B) electrodeposition potential, (C) concentration of Hg2+ aptamer, (D) incubation time between the NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt and AuNPs, (E) binding time of Hg2+, (F) pH value of buffer solution.
(A) ECL responses to various concentrations of Hg2+. (B) The calibration curve of the ECL response as a function of the concentration of Hg2+.
(A) Selectivity of the aptasensor with different interferences, (B) Repeatability of Hg2+ detection by 6 parallel measurements, (C) Stability of Hg2+ detection in seven days.
Schematic illustration of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection based on NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs.
Comparison of analytical performance of several approaches reported for detection of Hg2+.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: SPCE, screen-printed carbon electrode; IC-DNA, internal calibration DNA; Exo, exonuclease; afGQDs, amino-functionalized graphene quantum dots; Au@wood, gold nanoparticles encapsulated in porous wood; SERS, surface-enhanced Raman scattering; NPs, nanoparticles; GO, graphene oxide; ERGO, electrochemically reduced graphene oxide.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Analysis of real samples with different concentrations of Hg2+ using the proposed aptasensor (n = 3) and ICP-MS methods.

head.ce:title: Sensitive and selective detection of Hg2+ in tap and canal water via self-enhanced ECL aptasensor based on NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Modern Agricultural Equipment and Technology, Ministry of Education, School of Agricultural Engineering, Jiangsu University, Zhenjiang, Jiangsu, 212013, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Modern Agricultural Equipment and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Key Laboratory of Modern Agricultural Equipment and Technology, Ministry of Education, School of Agricultural Engineering, Jiangsu University, Zhenjiang, Jiangsu 212013, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Bioengineering, School of Engineering and Applied Sciences, University of Pennsylvania, Philadelphia, PA, 19104, United States

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Engineering and Applied Sciences

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Bioengineering, School of Engineering and Applied Sciences, University of Pennsylvania, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania 19104, United States

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In the present study, a novel self-enhanced electrochemiluminescence (ECL) aptasensor which combined self-enhanced ECL composite as signal response element and aptamer as the specific recognition element was firstly proposed for the sensitive and selective detection of Hg2+. Innovatively, the luminophore Ru(bpy)3 2+-doped silica nanoparticles functionalized with 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (NH2–Ru@SiO2) and the co-reactant nitrogen doped graphene quantum dots (NGQDs) were bound together to form the self-enhanced ECL composite, NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs, by electrostatic adsorption. Satisfactorily, high and stable luminous efficiency of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs was obtained benefited from the short electron transfer distance and low energy loss of self-enhanced-typal ECL composite. For the fabrication of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection, the Hg2+ aptamer, of which one end was connected to NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs, and the other end was fixed on glass carbon electrode (GCE) surface through Au–S bond, was served as a bridge. Upon the addition of Hg2+, the aptamer was bent due to the formation of thymine-Hg2+-thymine (T-Hg2+-T) specific structure, which caused the self-enhanced ECL composite was close to the GCE surface. On this basis, a linearly enhanced ECL signal was acquired with the concentration of Hg2+ in the range of 5.0 × 10−11 M − 1.0 × 10−6 M with excellent selectivity, repeatability and stability in 3 min for each assay. In addition, the proposed aptasensor showed satisfying accuracy and practicability for Hg2+ analysis in tap and canal water verified by the inductively coupled plasma-mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) method.

ce:section.ce:para: Mercury ion (Hg2+), as one of the hazardous heavy metal ions, is the most stable form of inorganic mercury [1]. Hg2+ is widely present in soil and can enter plant by infiltrating nutrients. Meanwhile, Hg2+ is easily dispersed at the atmosphere in the form of steam under the movement of water circulation because they have the widest distribution and the greatest harm in water [2]. When the concentration of Hg2+ exceeds the standard value of 0.15 mg kg−1, the soil is easily methylated, which further leads to plant poisoning [3]. Additionally, high content of Hg2+ in river can contaminate aquatic organisms such as fish and shrimp, which will cause irreversible damage including limb degeneration and brain death for health by accumulating in human body through food chain [4]. Therefore, it is imperative to achieve efficient and rapid detection of Hg2+.

Widely used methods for Hg2+ detection include atomic absorption spectroscopy (AAS) [5], atomic fluorescence spectrometry (AFS) [6], inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) [7], etc. These methods can realize simultaneous detection of multiple targets and obtain efficient and accurate results. However, the instruments required for these methods are expensive and request specialized techniques. In recent years, some simple to operate and low in cost approaches such as electrochemistry (EC), fluorescence (FL) and electrochemiluminescence (ECL) methods have attracted extensive attention and application for Hg2+ analysis [8–10]. Among them, ECL analytical technique combines the advantages of electrochemistry and chemiluminescence, which possesses lower light background and interference than photometric method, and higher sensitivity and wider dynamic range than EC [11–13]. Especially, combined with the feature that Hg2+ can bind with thymine (T)-rich apatmer to form T-Hg2+-T structure, various T-rich apatmer-based ECL aptasensors have been extensively developed for sensitive and selective Hg2+ detection [14–17]. For example, Babamiri et al. proposed a Hg2+ detection strategy based on the ECL resonance energy transfer (ECL-RET) principle from CdTe@CdS quantum dots to gold nanoparticles. As a result, an ultrasensitive Hg2+ assay with the detection limit of 2 × 10−18 M was obtained [14]. Feng et al. fabricated an ECL aptasensor based on MIL-53(Al)@CdTe-polyethyleneimine. The T-rich aptamer and G-rich aptamer labeled with metal nanoparticles were applied to capture Hg2+ and Pb2+, respectively. The proposed aptasensor could measure Hg2+ and Pb2+ at the same single interface with high sensitivity and specificity with a wide linear range [17].

Up to now, Ruthenium (II) tris (bipyridine) (Ru(bpy)3 2+) and its derivatives remain the most popular ECL reagents due to their recyclability, high quantum yields, and suitability in different pH levels [18]. However, individual Ru(bpy)3 2+ only can deliver a very weak ECL signal so that co-reactant must be introduced for obtaining a higher intensity and boosting analytical performance. Generally, the co-reactant is usually directly added to the working buffer solution or altered on the sensing interface, resulting in a decreased luminous efficiency and a perceptible detection error because of the long distance between luminophor and co-reactant [19]. In order to further improve luminous efficiency, many self-enhanced ECL composites have been prepared for the sake of contracting the electron transfer distance and depreciating energy loss. In the self-enhanced ECL system, the ECL luminophore and the corresponding co-reactant are covalently bound in the same molecular structure to form a luminescence group [20,21]. Until now, Ru-based self-enhanced ECL composite materials are usually prepared by linking with some co-reactants, such as polyamidoamine (PAMAM) [22], tris(3-aminopropyl) amine (TAPA) [23], ampicillin (Amp) [24] and tin disulfide (SnS2) [25]. Recently, nitrogen doped graphene quantum dots (NGQDs) with the benefits of low cost and simple operation has become a new hot spot in the fields of environmental monitoring and biopharmaceuticals, etc. [26,27]. At present, it has been reported that NGQDs can be used as Ru(bpy)3 2+ co-reactant and enhance ECL performance [28]. However, NGQDs has not been reported as co-reactant for developing self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection.

In the present work, a sensitive self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection was firstly constructed. As shown in Scheme 1 A, NGQDs was selected as a new type of co-reactant to combine with Ru(bpy)3 2+-doped silica nanoparticles functionalized with 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (NH2–Ru@SiO2 NPs) through electrostatic adsorption. Consequently, a new self-enhanced ECL composite (NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs) was formed, which shortened the electron transfer distance and further improved the luminous efficiency of the ECL luminophore. For the construction of the aptasensor, glassy carbon electrode (GCE, 3 mm) was modified with AuNPs via electrodeposition technique firstly (Scheme 1B). Then, the thiol-altered aptamer was linked with self-enhanced ECL composite, and the other end was attached to the GCE surface through an Au–S bond interaction. Bridge structure using Hg2+ aptamer as media had been constructed. In the presence of Hg2+, a stem-loop structure was formed because the T-rich aptamer was transformed to T-Hg2+-T structure. Ultimately, the ECL strength increased when the self-enhanced ECL luminous complex was close to the GCE surface. Based on this, sensitive and selective detection of Hg2+ was achieved. The proposed self-enhanced ECL aptasensor exhibited excellent analytical properties such as wider linear range and lower detection limit. At the same time, in the detection of Hg2+ in tap water and grand canal water, the developed aptasensor showed advantage of good practical application.

Tris (2,2-bipyridyl) dichlororuthenium (II) hexahydrate (Ru(bpy)3Cl2·6H2O), 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES) were supplied by Sigma-Aldrich. Bovine serum albumin (BSA) was purchased from Adamas Reagent. Triton X-100 was obtained from Alfa Aesar. Tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS), tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane hydrochloride (tris-HCl) were purchased from Aladdin. Ammonium citrate tribasic, ammonia solution, cyclohexane, acetone, disodium hydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate (Na2HPO4·12H2O), sodium dihydrogen phosphate dehydrates (NaH2PO4·2H2O), ethanol absolute, sodium chloride and sulfuric acid were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). N-hexanol was acquired from URChem Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine hydrochloride (TCEP), ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid disodium salt (EDTA) were supplied bydamas-beta. Tetrachloroauric (Ⅲ) acid trihydrate was purchased from Acros Organics. 1-(3-Dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimidehydrochloride (EDC) and N-Hydroxysuccinimide (NHS) were acquired from TCI. Hg(NO3)2·H2O was obtained from Tongren Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Guizhou, China). Hg2+ aptamer (5′-NH2-TTGTTTGTCCCCTCTTTCTTA-(CH2)3-SH-3′) were obtained from Sangon Biotech Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). All other reagents were analytical grade and used as received. The aqueous solutions applied in all experiments were used by ultrapure water (18.2 MΩ). In addition, the aptamer buffer solution was received by dissolving aptamer in Tris-HCl (pH 7.4). 0.1 M phosphate buffered solution (PBS) was prepared through dissolving 0.1 M Na2HPO4 and NaH2PO4.

Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and SEM images were characterized by JEOL 2100 instrument (JEOL, Japan) and Inspect F50 (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA), respectively. The zeta potential was conducted by a Zetasizer Nano analyzer (Malvern, UK). Ultraviolet–visible (UV–Vis) spectra and fluorescence (FL) spectra were performed by UV-2450 spectrophotometer (Shimadzu, Japan) and FS5 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Edinburgh Instruments, UK), respectively. ICP-MS was tested under iCAP™ RQ ICP-MS (Thermo Fisher Scientific, U.S.). Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) was conducted using ZENNIUM electrochemical workstation (Zahner Instruments, Germany). All the electrochemical (EC) and electrochemiluminescent (ECL) signal were recorded on MPI-EII multifunction chemiluminescence detector (Xi'an Remax Electronic Science & Technology Co. Ltd., China). In addition, all experiments were conducted on three-electrode system, where modified glass carbon electrode (GCE, 3 mm) was used as working electrode, Ag/AgCl (saturated KCl) electrode as reference electrode and platinum wire as counter electrode, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs self-enhanced ECL composite
ce:section.ce:para: Based on the electrostatic interaction between positively charged NH2–Ru@SiO2 and negatively charged NGQDs, self-enhanced ECL composite (NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs) was prepared according to previous reports [28]. Among them, NGQDs and NH2–Ru@SiO2 were respectively synthesized from previously reported literature with a little modification (see Support Information). For the preparation of the self-enhanced ECL composite, 5 mL 5 mg mL−1 NGQDs and 1 mL 1 mg mL−1 NH2–Ru@SiO2 were mixed and stirred for 12 h. After that, the self-enhanced ECL composite NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs was centrifuged at 10000 rpm for 10 min to remove insoluble impurities. The obtained self-enhanced ECL composite was placed at 4 °C until use.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Modification of the self-enhanced ECL composite (NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs) with Hg2+ aptamer
ce:section.ce:para: According to previously published literature with some modification [29], the obtained 800 μL NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs was mixed with 400 μL 10 mM PBS (pH 7.4) including 10 mM EDC and 5 mM NHS to activate carboxy groups upon NGQDs by stirring for 15 min. Then the condensation reaction would take place with the addition of 800 μL 2.5 μM Hg2+ aptamer. Light yellow solution was received after continuously stirring 2 h. All solutions were centrifuged with a speed of 10000 rpm for 10 min. Finally, the self-enhanced luminous complex NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt was achieved.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fabrication of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection
ce:section.ce:para: The construction process of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor based on the NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs composite was shown in Scheme 1B. Before the modification, the GCE was polished sequentially by 0.3 μm and 0.05 μm alumina power, and washed thoroughly using alcohol and water, respectively. Then, the GCE was immersed in 1% HAuCl4 solution and electrodeposited at −0.25 V for 20 s to generate AuNPs substrate on the GCE surface. Sequentially, 6 μL NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt was immobilized onto the AuNPs/GCE surface immediately through Au–S bond and incubated for 10 h at 4 °C. After removing the unbound aptamer with ultrapure water, the modified GCE was soaked in BSA (80 μL, 1%) solution for 1 h at 37 °C to block the nonspecific binding sites. Ultimately, the GCE was immersed in Hg2+ solution with various concentrations. The content of Hg2+ could be detected by the change of the ECL signal when the self-enhanced composite was closed to the GCE surface due to the formation of T-Hg2+-T structure.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Morphological and structural characterization of self-enhanced ECL composite
ce:section.ce:para: The morphology of the NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs was characterized by TEM measurement. Fig. 1 A shows that NGQDs with the diameter of 2.33 nm is closely and uniformly attached to NH2–Ru@SiO2 surface and the average size of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs is 49.3 ± 3.0 nm, which preliminarily proves that the composite material is successfully prepared. The zeta potential results of different nanomaterials (Fig. 1B) exhibit that the electric charge of Ru@SiO2 changes from −3.81 mV to 11.3 mV due to the amination of Ru@SiO2 using APTES to form NH2–Ru@SiO2. Upon adding negative NGQDs (−12.9 mV), zeta potential of NH2–Ru@SiO2 changes from positive to negative −8.98 mV, further proving that the self-enhanced ECL composite NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs is synthesized successfully.

Optical properties of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs were investigated by UV–Vis absorption spectroscopy and fluorescence spectroscopy. UV–Vis absorption spectra (Fig. 1C) indicate that the pure NGQDs (Fig. 1C, line a) show one remarkable peak at 366 nm assigning to the π-π* conjugate structures of aromatic C–C domains of NGQDs [30]. For the both Ru@SiO2 (Fig. 1C, line b) and NH2–Ru@SiO2 (Fig. 1C, line c), two prominent peaks at 286 nm and 453 nm are observed ascribing to respective metal-to-ligand charge transfer (MLCT) of Ru(bpy)3 2+ molecules and ligand-centered transitions, which indicates that Ru(bpy)3 2+ is successfully encapsulated in SiO2 NPs, and the amination has no effect on its properties [31]. The characteristic peaks of NH2–Ru@SiO2 at 286 nm, 453 nm can be observed from the UV–Vis absorbance of NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs (Fig. 1C, line d), the simultaneous existence of NGQDs at 366 nm is confirmed by zooming in (Fig. 1C, inset), indicating the successful preparation of the self-enhanced ECL composite.

Fluorescence technology (Fig. 1D) is another essential method for material characterization. The spectrum belonging to NGQDs at 420 nm is clearly observed from Fig. 1D, line a. At the same time, both Ru@SiO2 (Fig. 1D, line b) and NH2–Ru@SiO2 (Fig. 1D, line c) exhibit similar fluorescence spectra at 621 nm corresponding to Ru(bpy)3 2+ molecules [32], which imply that Ru(bpy)3 2+ is encapsulated into the SiO2 NPs successfully. Upon the introduction of NGQDs (Fig. 1D, line d), there are two emission wavelengths at 415 nm and 617 nm attributing to NGQDs and NH2–Ru@SiO2 NPs respectively. These results consist well with those of UV–Vis absorption spectra, further demonstrating the successful combination of NH2–Ru@SiO2 NPs with NGQDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fabrication of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor and feasibility study for Hg2+ detection
ce:section.ce:para: The construction process of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor for Hg2+ detection was monitored by cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). As shown in Fig. 2 A, a pair of obvious redox peaks at 0.28 V and 0.19 V appear in 5 mM [Fe(CN)6]3-/4- with 0.2 M KCl from the bare GCE (Fig. 2A, line a). When AuNPs are electrodeposited on GCE surface, the redox current increases clearly (Fig. 2A, line b) owning to the superior conductivity of AuNPs [33]. However, when self-enhanced luminous complex NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt is embellished on AuNPs/GCE, the redox current decreases (Fig. 2A, line c) because the aptamer is not conductive and hinders electron transfer rate. When BSA is used to block the nonspecific binding sites, the current value dramatically decreases (Fig. 2A, line d). These results indicate that self-enhanced ECL aptasensor is successfully fabricated.

By the same token, EIS was used to characterize the modification process of GCE surface (Fig. 2B), and its main fitting parameters include the electrolyte solution (Rs), constant phase element (Q), the resistance of electron transfer (Ret) and Warburg impedance (Zw) (Inset of Fig. 2B). The Ret value of bare GCE is 103 Ω (Fig. 2B, line a). When AuNPs is attached on the GCE surface, the Ret value drops to 45 Ω, corroborating the successful loading of AuNPs (Fig. 2B, line b). After the immobilization of complex NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt (Fig. 2B, line c) and BSA (Fig. 2B, line d), the Ret value increases significantly to 143 Ω and 364 Ω due to the poor conductive characteristics of biological macromolecules. These EIS responses correspond to the conclusions of CV, which further demonstrates that the manufacture of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor is triumphant.

Next, the feasibility of the self-enhanced aptasensor for Hg2+ detection was corroborated by ECL technique (Fig. 2C). Neither the bare GCE (Fig. 2C, line a) nor the AuNPs/GCE (Fig. 2C, line b) show obvious ECL signal because of the absence of self-enhanced luminous complex. When NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt is assembled on AuNPs/GCE surface via Au–S bond, the self-enhanced ECL composite NH2–Ru@ SiO2-NGQDs shows a clear ECL signal of 761 a.u. (Fig. 2C, line c). After that, BSA (1%) is incubated with the as-prepared GCE to block the non-specific sites, leading to a reduced ECL signal of 457 a.u (Fig. 2C, line d) owning to the inferior conductivity from biomolecules. When the BSA/NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt/AuNPs/GCE is incubated with Hg2+ (10−8 M), the signal of ECL boosts up to 2458 a.u. (line e) eventually. These results indicate that Hg2+ can be detected by the arranged self-enhanced ECL aptasensor successfully.

For the sake of escalating the detection peculiarities of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor, electrodeposition time and potential of AuNPs, concentration of Hg2+ aptamer, incubation time between NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt and AuNPs, binding time of Hg2+ and pH value of buffer solution were optimized in detail.

Influence of the electrodeposition time is displayed in Fig. 3 A. The ECL signal increases significantly with the extension of the electrodeposition time to 20 s, indicating that the AuNPs is successfully loaded. And the more the amount, the larger the ECL signal. However, as time goes on, AuNPs may become larger to form gold film, which can suppress the electron transfer rate and weakens the ECL signal [34,35]. Meanwhile, electrodeposition voltage is another important factor in the formation of AuNPs. When the voltage is too negative to −0.4 V, the amount of AuNPs increases and cannot play the role of good conductivity. When the voltage increases to −0.1 V, the formation of AuNPs will be insufficient. The strongest ECL intensity is achieved when −0.25 V potential of electrodeposition is applied (Fig. 3B). As a result, the electrodeposition conditions are set as −0.25 V with 20 s.

It can be seen from Fig. 3C that the concentration of aptamer is amended from 0.1 μM to 3 μM. The aptamer is composited with self-enhanced ECL composite by modifying the amino at one end, and the other end decorated with thiol to form a bridge structure when connected with AuNPs. As the concentration increases, the ECL intensity enhances with the more self-enhanced ECL composite linked. Then ECL intensity becomes stable upon higher concentration, which probably attributes to saturation. Therefore, 2.5 μM aptamer can connect the most self-enhanced ECL composite so that the ECL emission strength is maximized and stable.

Next, the incubation time between NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt and AuNPs was revised from 4 h to 14 h which displayed in Fig. 3D. When the time prolongs from 4 h to 8 h, the more AuNPs is combined with NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt, the higher ECL strength increases significantly. After 8 h, the ECL intensity is almost unchanged, indicating that the binding is absolute. For more permanent binding, 10 h is the most reasonable incubation time.

The binding time of Hg2+ was also studied as demonstrated in Fig. 3E. The prepared aptasensor was incubated by 10−8 M Hg2+ for various time intervals. As the binding time increases from 40 min to 70 min, the ECL signal elevates extremely, indicating the generation of the T-Hg2+-T structure. Afterwards, ECL intensity tends to be a steady platform upon 70 min, indicating the binding of Hg2+ to the aptamer is completed. Hence, 90 min can be regarded as the binding time.

The pH value in buffer solution is a key parameter that can affect the structure and performance of Hg2+ aptamer. In Fig. 3F, the effect of pH in the PBS is studied from 6.5 to 8.5. The ECL response increases significantly as the pH value changes from 6.5 to 7.5, and abates as the pH transforms to 8.5, which may be the fact that too high or too low pH will make the structure and performance destroyed and the affinity between AuNPs and aptamer further reduces [36]. Therefore, the buffer solution with pH of 7.5 is selected.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analytical performance of the self-enhanced ECL aptaseneor for Hg2+ detection
ce:section.ce:para: Under the conditions of optimization, the analytical performance of the proposed aptasensor for Hg2+ detection was examined. From the diagram Fig. 4 A, as Hg2+ concentration expands, the number of self-enhanced ECL composite approaching the surface of GCE increases, and the ECL signal amplifies eventually. Accordingly, the ECL intensity is directly proportional to the logarithm of the Hg2+ concentration ranging from 5.0 × 10−11 M to 1.0 × 10−6 M (Fig. 4B) with the limit of detection (LOD) of 3.0 × 10−11 M and limit of quantification (LOQ) of 3.88 × 10−11 M according to the IUPAC definition [37]. The linear regression equation is I = 559 logCHg 2+ + 6323 with a correlation coefficient of 0.9959.

In order to reflect the good performance of the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor clearly, we compared it with some previously reported approaches for detecting Hg2+ (Table 1 ). It can be observed that the analytical performance of the proposed ECL aptasensor is comparable to those of EC methods based on different strategy [38–40] from the linear range angle. Compared with those photometric methods including colorimetry [41,42], SERS [43] and fluorescence [44], the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor not only obtains considerable detection limit, but also exhibits more wider linear range with five orders of magnitude, which is favorable to achieve the detection of trace concentrations and directly detect severely contaminated samples. Finally, compared with similar ECL biosensors based on different materials [45–47], it can be clearly seen that the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor is the most sensitive, the material performance is more stable, and the operation process is simpler. This shows that the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor achieves ideal performance and has great application prospects.

The specificity of the constructed aptasensor for Hg2+ detection was investigated by combining NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-apt with different metal ions, including Fe2+, K+, Mg2+, Na+, Fe3+, Zn2+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Mn2+, Cu2+, Pb2+ at the concentration of 5.0 × 10−6 M. As shown in Fig. 5 A, upon the addition of interfering ions, tiny ECL responses were induced. While 1.0 × 10−7 M of Hg2+ can lead to significantly increased ECL intensity (ΔIECL) of 1624 a.u., where ΔIECL = I – I0, and I and I0 represent the ECL signal intensity in the presence and absence of metal ions, respectively. Besides, the mixture with the presence of Hg2+ (1.0 × 10−7 M) shows obvious enhancement of ECL signal, agreeing well with pure Hg2+ solution. The above results illustrate that the specific detection of Hg2+ can be realized by the self-enhanced ECL aptasensor.

Repeatability of the ECL aptasensor was investigated by parallel fabrication of six sets of aptasensor (Fig. 5B). When six sets of ECL aptasensors are applied to detect Hg2+ at the same concentration, the relative standard deviation (RSD) is determined to be 1.09%, indicating good fabrication repeatability of the ECL aptasensor. In addition, the stability was researched by ECL signals generated by aptasensor placed under the same conditions for different times. The ECL responses show that the strength of the seventh day maintains 97% of initial data, suggesting that the aptasensor is extremely stable (Fig. 5C).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of Hg2+ in tap and the grand canal water
ce:section.ce:para: Tap water is related to people's direct domestic water, and the grand canal is the main source water of transportation and irrigation. Therefore, the application of self-enhanced ECL aptasensor in actual samples was investigated. Good recoveries (between 93.3% and 101%) are obtained, which is acceptable with a satisfactory RSD from 0.13% to 1.33% (Table 2 ). The reliability of the aptasensor was verified with national standard method inductively coupled plasma-mass spectrometry (ICP-MS). The recoveries obtained from ICP-MS are between 94.0% and 107% (Table 2), which is in good agreement with the proposed ECL aptasensor. These results show that the NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs-based self-enhanced ECL aptasensor can detect Hg2+ accurately in different water bodies.

Generally, a sensitive self-enhanced ECL aptasensor based on NH2–Ru@SiO2-NGQDs was firstly prepared for the detection of Hg2+. NGQDs was used as an effective co-reactant of NH2–Ru@SiO2 to prepare a self-enhanced ECL composite, which decreased the electron transfer distance and greatly amplified ECL intensity. In addition, Hg2+ could specifically bind with its aptamer to form T-Hg2+-T structure. Therefore, a self-enhanced ECL sensing platform was constructed to detect Hg2+. It turned out that the aptasensor had good selectivity and high sensitivity. Finally, the good recoveries obtained from actual samples detection proved that this aptasensor could provide an advanced technique for the detection of heavy metal ions.

Libo Li: Conceptualization, Investigation, Writing - original draft & Writing - review & editing, Funding acquisition. Bainian Chen: Investigation, Data curation, Software, Writing - original draft. Lijun Luo: Software, Writing - review & editing. Xiaohong Liu: Software, Writing - review & editing. Xiaoya Bi: Software, Writing - review & editing. Tianyan You: Writing - review & editing, Supervision, Funding acquisition.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This project was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21675065, 61801195), China Postdoctoral Science Foundation (2019T120399, 2018M632238), the Innovation/Entrepreneurship Program of Jiangsu Province and the Priority Academic Program Development (PAPD) of Jiangsu Higher Education Institutions (No. PAPD-2018-87).

ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the Supplementary data to this article:

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.talanta.2020.121579.
